title: planchonella umbonata
intro: Planchonella umbonata, also known as the Marama or Black Sapote, is a tropical tree species native to the Pacific Islands, characterized by its dark brown, edible fruit. The Marama tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to improve cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Planchonella umbonata include tinctures, infusions, and decoctions, often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
